title: Return on Assets
event: Reyes Co. reports net income of $40,000. Total assets were $380,000 at the beginning of the year and $420,000 at year-end. Compute return on assets.

computationPaths.narration: ROA measures how much income each dollar of assets produced — and it uses AVERAGE assets, since income was earned across the whole year.

memorize.body: ROA = Net income ÷ AVERAGE total assets. Average = (beginning + ending) ÷ 2.
Any ratio pairing an income-statement number (a flow) with a balance-sheet number (a snapshot) uses the AVERAGE snapshot.

distractors.feedback: You divided by ENDING assets. Income was earned all year, so use the average base: 400,000 → 10%.
You divided by BEGINNING assets. Average the two snapshots first: (380 + 420) ÷ 2 = 400.
